title: salix retusa var. major
intro: Salix retusa var. major, also known as the dwarf willow or Salix retusa, is a variety of the Salix retusa shrub, typically found in mountainous regions with cool and moist climates. The bark and leaves of the Salix retusa var. major plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which help in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and soothing digestive issues, due to its rich content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The Salix retusa var. major plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
